claim: Elevation-type BIPV optimizer study reports optimizer-equipped BIPV had about 2-3x lower power-loss rate than non-optimized BIPV under about 10-75% shade on one module.

claim: Study system used 171 W modules, 20.77 V maximum power voltage, 342 W system capacity, south/90 degree orientation.

claim: Optimizer used in study had 600 W rated input DC power, 60 V maximum input voltage, 8-60 V MPPT operating range, and 150x100x42 mm dimensions.

claim: Facade-BIPV study developed/tested a prototype optimizer and compared no optimizer, conventional optimizer, and prototype optimizer under 10%, 25%, 50%, and 75% one-module shading.

claim: Facade-BIPV study reports PV energy at 75% one-module shading of 0.34 kWh/day without optimizer versus 0.65 kWh/day with optimizers in the scraped table.

claim: IEA PVPS Task 15 report says BIPV often needs higher spatial-temporal granularity because non-optimal placements are more common than in solar power plants.

claim: IEA PVPS report states many BIPV tools still lack important features for vertical or externally mounted BIPV.
